source.description: User-controlled URL string bound directly from the HTTP query parameter via @RequestParam. Spring performs no sanitization during binding.

propagation.description: The only check is an isEmpty() guard that rejects blank strings. No scheme, host, or IP-range validation is applied; the url value reaches the HTTP client unchanged.

sink.description: RestTemplate.getForObject() initiates an outbound HTTP request to the attacker-controlled URL. Internal addresses such as http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ are reachable.

expected_findings.risk_reasoning: The url parameter flows from the HTTP request directly into RestTemplate.getForObject() with no validation. An attacker can target internal services, cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), or arbitrary internet hosts.
expected_findings.remediation_hint: Parse the URL with java.net.URL, enforce an https-only scheme allowlist, restrict hostnames to a known domain allowlist, and block private/loopback/link-local IP ranges. Configure RestTemplate with setInstanceFollowRedirects(false).

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink where the unsanitized URL enters RestTemplate.getForObject().
